summary: Percent-encodes a value into a URL-safe string, with alternate handling when ISO-8859-1 output is requested.
signature_intent: Accepts a value, an unused encoder argument, and an optional character set indicator; returns either the original empty value or an encoded string.
behavior: Returns the original value unchanged when its length is zero.
Converts symbols using their symbol string form and other non-string values using standard string conversion.
In ISO-8859-1 mode, applies legacy percent escaping and converts non-Latin-1 Unicode escapes into numeric character references that are themselves percent-encoded.
In the default mode, leaves ASCII letters, digits, hyphen, period, underscore, and tilde unescaped.
In the default mode, percent-encodes other ASCII characters as single bytes.
In the default mode, percent-encodes non-ASCII characters as UTF-8 bytes.
Encodes valid surrogate pairs as four-byte UTF-8 sequences.
edge_cases: Null or undefined input throws before conversion because length is read first.
Objects with zero length are returned unchanged instead of being stringified.
Lone surrogate code units are treated as if paired with the following code unit, which can produce nonstandard encoding.
The supplied encoder callback has no observable effect.
